incidents.victim: Microsoft Windows and SharePoint Server users globally
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, a SharePoint Server spoofing vulnerability, alongside public exploit code for CVE-2026-33825 (Windows Defender privilege escalation dubbed 'BlueHammer') in Microsoft's 167-vulnerability April Patch Tuesday.
incidents.impact: Attackers are actively spoofing trusted content in SharePoint environments to enable phishing and unauthorized data manipulation. The BlueHammer exploit code was publicly released after researcher frustration with Microsoft's response timeline.

incidents.vector: Human error during a planned system update exposed protected address data in domestic payment transaction details visible to payment recipients.
incidents.impact: Approximately 20,600 client addresses, including protected addresses, were inadvertently disclosed to third parties. The bank says only recipients who opened detailed payment information could see the data.

incidents.victim: Cloud workloads on AWS, GCP, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud environments
incidents.vector: Deployed an undetectable ELF backdoor on Linux cloud instances using SMTP port 25 as a covert C2 channel, with typosquatted domains mimicking Alibaba Cloud services registered in a 24-hour burst window.
incidents.impact: Backdoor harvests temporary cloud credentials from AWS instance metadata service and equivalent endpoints for GCP, Azure, and Alibaba Cloud. Zero detections on VirusTotal at time of analysis, representing six years of investment in cloud-native tooling.

incidents.vector: Breached third-party analytics platform Anodot to gain access to Rockstar's Snowflake cloud data warehouse account, exfiltrating 78 million records.
incidents.impact: Stolen data reportedly includes GTA Online and Red Dead Online in-game revenue metrics, player behavior tracking data, and game economy data. Snowflake confirmed the breach originated from Anodot, not its own platform, and preventively disabled all Anodot-linked accounts.

incidents.victim: Adobe Acrobat and Reader users globally
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-34621 (CVSS 8.6) via maliciously crafted PDF files that execute immediately upon opening, fingerprinting victims before deciding whether to deploy follow-up RCE/sandbox escape payloads.
incidents.impact: Zero-day was actively exploited since at least November 2025, going largely undetected for four months. The malware silently exfiltrates OS details, software versions, file paths, and local files to attacker-controlled C2 infrastructure. Patched April 11.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers that executes a wiper payload on systems matching Iran's timezone or Farsi language settings.
incidents.impact: Data on infected Iranian systems is wiped. The same TeamPCP infrastructure was previously used in the Trivy supply chain attack. The wiper campaign appears opportunistically tied to the ongoing US-Iran military conflict.

incidents.victim: Government agencies, military, and critical infrastructure across 120+ countries
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, MikroTik) to hijack DNS settings, redirecting traffic through attacker-controlled servers to intercept OAuth tokens and credentials via adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Microsoft Outlook Web Access.
incidents.impact: Over 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices compromised. Tokens harvested bypassed MFA, granting direct account access. FBI conducted court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' to remediate compromised U.S. routers. U.K. NCSC issued parallel advisory.

incidents.victim: U.S. water, energy, and municipal government systems
incidents.vector: Exploited internet-facing operational technology devices, primarily Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley PLCs (CVE-2021-22681) and Siemens equipment, to manipulate SCADA/HMI displays and disrupt PLC functions.
incidents.impact: Multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors experienced operational disruptions including manipulated control displays. A water treatment plant in Minot, North Dakota reported a ransomware-like attack. FBI confirmed investigations into attacks in North Dakota and Indiana. Activity escalated in response to the US-Iran military conflict.

incidents.vector: Chained multiple security bypasses in Grafana's AI components — including domain validation flaws and prompt injection that circumvented model guardrails — to silently exfiltrate sensitive data without authentication or user interaction.
incidents.impact: The 'GrafanaGhost' vulnerability could expose financial metrics, customer records, and infrastructure telemetry stored in Grafana dashboards. Grafana Labs issued a fix but disputes the zero-click characterization. No evidence of in-the-wild exploitation.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.8), an unauthenticated remote code execution zero-day in FortiClient EMS. Initial exploitation observed March 31, ramping up after Fortinet's hotfix disclosure on April 6.
incidents.impact: Nearly 2,000 publicly exposed FortiClient EMS instances identified. This is the second critical FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks. CISA added it to the KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch. Full patch still pending — only a hotfix is available.

incidents.victim: University of Mississippi Medical Center, Passaic County NJ, and other healthcare and government organizations
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware operators exploited zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-23760 in SmarterMail, CVE-2025-10035 in GoAnywhere MFT) days before public disclosure, achieving data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours of initial access.
incidents.impact: The largest hospital in Mississippi was forced to fully close and required FBI and DHS assistance to reopen on March 2. Medusa is increasingly targeting healthcare and municipal governments. Symantec also observed North Korean operators deploying Medusa ransomware.

incidents.vector: German Federal Criminal Police (BKA) identified 31-year-old Russian Daniil Shchukin as the leader of both GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations, which pioneered double extortion tactics.
incidents.impact: Shchukin and co-conspirator Anatoly Kravchuk are linked to 130+ acts of computer sabotage, $2 billion+ in extortion across GandCrab/REvil operations, and 35 million euros in damages in Germany alone. Shchukin is believed to reside in Russia.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads through a compromised on-premises government server, deploying the Havoc penetration testing tool and ShadowPad malware.
incidents.impact: A single compromised TrueConf server serving dozens of government entities delivered weaponized updates to all connected agencies. CISA ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch by April 16. TrueConf serves approximately 100,000 organizations globally, primarily in government and military sectors.

incidents.victim: Mercor, OpenAI, and thousands of organizations using Axios JavaScript library
incidents.vector: Hijacked the lead Axios maintainer's npm account via social engineering, publishing malicious versions (v1.14.1, v0.30.4) containing a cross-platform RAT disguised as 'plain-crypto-js' dependency, affecting Windows, macOS, and Linux.
incidents.impact: Axios is downloaded over 100 million times weekly, creating massive downstream exposure. OpenAI confirmed impact to its macOS certificate signing workflow and is revoking certificates by May 8. Mercor ($10B valuation AI recruiting firm) confirmed breach. Multiple sectors affected across U.S., Europe, Middle East, and Australia.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP compromised a LiteLLM maintainer's PyPI account and published malicious package versions, injecting credential-stealing code into the open-source AI proxy library's supply chain.
incidents.impact: Thousands of firms potentially impacted. Lapsus$ separately claimed to have obtained hundreds of gigabytes of Mercor data. A clean version of LiteLLM was released after the compromise was discovered. Attack is part of a broader TeamPCP campaign targeting cloud infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-5281, a use-after-free vulnerability in Chrome's Dawn WebGPU implementation, marking the fourth Chrome zero-day of 2026.
incidents.impact: CISA added the vulnerability to its KEV catalog based on evidence of active exploitation. Google released an emergency Chrome update. The accelerating pace of Chrome zero-days (four in less than four months) signals increased targeting of browser attack surfaces.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP executed a supply chain attack against the Trivy vulnerability scanner by injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases via compromised GitHub Actions workflows.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions exfiltrated SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and cryptocurrency wallets from users before Aqua Security removed the harmful files. The same TeamPCP infrastructure was later reused for the CanisterWorm wiper targeting Iran.
